summary: Comprehensive backend testing of 'Who I'm open to meeting' dating compatibility matching logic. All 22 tests passed. The check_dating_compatibility function correctly filters discovery results based on user intent, gender, and who_open_to_meeting preferences.

friends_intent_no_filter.description: Users with 'friends' intent see ALL visible users - no dating filter applied

dating_intent_filters_friends_only.description: Dating intent users do NOT see friends-only users

bidirectional_compatibility.description: Both users' preferences must match for compatibility (e.g., Demo seeking women + Emma seeking men = compatible)

incompatible_preferences_filtered.description: Users with incompatible preferences are correctly filtered (e.g., Maya seeking women not visible to Demo who is a man)

everyone_preference_compatible.description: Users with 'everyone' preference are compatible with all genders (but still subject to bidirectional check)

critical_code_review_comments: check_dating_compatibility function (line 839) correctly implements bidirectional matching logic
All three discovery endpoints (/discovery/not-here, /discovery/here, /venues/{id}/people) apply the filter
Friends intent users bypass the dating filter completely (correct behavior)
Empty gender or empty who_open_to_meeting defaults to compatible (backward compatibility)
seed_data_creation: Created 5 new test users with valid email domains (@example.com) and a test venue with checkins for all users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dating compatibility matching logic fully tested. Test users created: emma.dating.test@example.com (woman seeking men), james.dating.test@example.com (man seeking women), maya.dating.test@example.com (woman seeking women), suzy.friends.test@example.com (friends only), alex.everyone.test@example.com (seeking everyone). All users have locations set near London (51.5xxx, -0.1xxx) and are checked into test-venue-dating-filter. The check_dating_compatibility function at line 839 in server.py handles all the filtering logic.
